channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Duke's Cooper Flagg starts fast and finishes with an ACC freshman scoring record
item.description: DURHAM, N.C. (AP) — Cooper Flagg cut across the court to catch an early kickout feed and knocked down a 3-pointer from the wing. Moments later, he was alone in the left corner and confidently had a catch-and-score for another 3. No hesitation.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: ESPN airs pre-game prayer for Cotton Bowl after backlash for not showing national anthem after terror attack
item.description: ESPN's coverage of the Cotton Bowl included the airing of a prayer on the field before the game after a week of controversy for not airing the national anthem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Giants legend Victor Cruz talks state of team after it retained coach, general manager despite abysmal season
item.description: The New York Giants face maybe the most important offseason in a generation, and Victor Cruz suggested what they should do in the next few months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lindsey Vonn impresses skiing into 6th place in her first World Cup downhill race since 2019
item.description: Lindsey Vonn has skied into an impressive sixth place in her first World Cup downhill race for six years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (AP) — Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died. He was 84. The charismatic figure known as Coach Mac died Friday night “after a courageous journey with dementia,” according to a family statement.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio State's late scoop-and-score catapults Buckeyes to national championship over Texas
item.description: Jack Sawyer's 82-yard scoop and score made him an Ohio State legend and helped the Buckeyes to a win over Texas to go to the national championship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio State, Notre Dame to meet for title, long after upset losses
item.description: Two upset losses stood out among the rest in college football this season. Notre Dame suffered one of them. Ohio State the other. In years past, those losses might have been enough to knock both teams out of the hunt for a national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lakers coach JJ Redick confirms his family's home was lost in the Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: EL SEGUNDO, Calif. (AP) — Los Angeles Lakers head coach JJ Redick confirmed his family lost its home in the raging wildfire in Pacific Palisades this week.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rose Bowl half-marathon and 5K postponed due to wildfires
item.description: The annual Rose Bowl half-marathon and 5K, originally scheduled for Jan. 19, has been postponed as wildfires continue to ravage the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former WWE CEO Vince McMahon, Securities and Exchange Commission reach settlement after lengthy probe
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission said it reached a settlement agreement with WWE co-founder Vince McMahon, which requires he pay a civil penalty and reimburse the WWE $1.33 million following a yearslong probe over administrative charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cardinals send team planes to evacuate Rams' players, families and pets from wildfires: report
item.description: The Arizona Cardinals are reportedly sending two of their team planes to Los Angeles to evacuate LA Rams players, their families, team staffers and pets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles QB Jalen Hurts clears concussion protocol and will start wild-card game against Green Bay
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Out of NFL concussion protocol, Jalen Hurts is ready to roll for the Eagles. With Hurts cleared, the NFC East champion Eagles (14-3) are close to full strength in the starting lineup for their NFC wild-card game Sunday against Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago Public Schools paid $390K to do-not-hire sports officials
item.description: Chicago Public Schools paid $390,000 to at least 36 people who were on a do-not-hire list to officiate sporting events.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Eagles' Jalen Hurts says he doesn't remember concussion sideline incident, amid bad year for brain injuries
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts told reporters on Friday that he doesn't remember what happened after he suffered the concussion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lamar Jackson repeats and Ja’Marr Chase and Justin Jefferson are unanimous choices for AP All-Pro
item.description: Lamar Jackson beat out Josh Allen, and Ja’Marr Chase and Justin Jefferson are unanimous choices for The Associated Press 2024 NFL All-Pro Team. Saquon Barkley received 48 of 50 first-place votes and Patrick Surtain II got 49 from a nationwide panel of media members who regularly cover the league.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL moves Vikings-Rams playoff game to Arizona after days of devastating wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The NFL has moved the Rams' wild-card playoff game against the Minnesota Vikings to Arizona after days of devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area. The game will be played Monday night at State Farm Stadium, the home of the Arizona Cardinals, in suburban Glendale.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google Trends: Nearly every US state is searching the Broncos over the Bills
item.description: We looked at Google Trends statistics to see whether the Broncos or the Bills were the talk of the country – and one team won by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, Fox 5

item.title: Brayden Point reaches 600 career points in Lightning's 4-1 victory over the Bruins
item.description: Brayden Point reached 600 career points with a power-play goal, Andrei Vasilevskiy made 27 saves, and the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the Boston Bruins 4-1 on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Raider Charles Snowden thought he was in Florida during DUI arrest: Video
item.description: Tests showed Snowden’s blood-alcohol level was more than double the legal limit after officers found him asleep and unresponsive in a running SUV, documents show.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Utah HC loses to Stanley Cup champs, 4-1
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club continues to struggle at home, losing its fourth straight game at the Delta Center Wednesday night to the defending Stanley Cup champion Florida Panthers, 4-1.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: 6 Bulls earn CSC Academic All-District honors
item.description: The University of South Florida football program had six student-athletes earn recognition on the 2024 Academic All-District Football Team.
item.dc:creator: Brian Siegrist, USF Associate AD, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bears interview Mike Vrabel for head coaching position; Iowa State's Matt Campbell also comes up, report says
item.description: The Bears interviewed former Tennessee Titans head coach Mike Vrabel for their head coaching job on Wednesday, which was not unexpected. But a name from the college ranks that has now come up, according to a Chicago Tribune report, certainly wasn't on the radar of NFL coaching carousel pundits.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls fall to Pacers, 2-game winning streak snapped as longest homestand of season begins
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — The Bulls were looking to go into their longest homestand of the season on a three-game winning streak. Instead, they'll be trying to start a new one.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Basketball: University City 61, Lincoln 59
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- What a game on the hardwood at University City in boys action with the Centurions beating Lincoln in double overtime 61-59. Dylan Griffin paced the Centurions with a game high 38 points.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Thune tees up Tuberville's bill to block transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Tuesday took the necessary procedural steps to place Sen. Tommy Tuberville’s (R-Ala.) bill blocking transgender athletes from women’s sports on the Senate calendar. The step sets up a vote on Tuberville’s bill in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Emeka Eneli, Diego Luna called to USMNT Camp after breakout seasons
item.description: Real Salt Lake midfielder Emeka Eneli and Diego Luna's stellar 2024 MLS campaigns have been rewarded with call-ups to the U.S. Men's National Team's January training camp, making a pivotal moment in their burgeoning careers.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Former Baltimore Orioles left-hander Brian Matusz, who made final MLB appearance with Cubs in 2016, dies at 37
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Brian Matusz, the left-handed pitcher who spent eight seasons in the major leagues and made his final big-league appearance with the Cubs during their 2016 World Series championship season, has died. He was 37. Matusz, a former No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz lose on half-court shot at the buzzer
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz' attempt at its first three-game winning streak of the season ended in heartbreaking fashion. Trae Young hit a buzzer-beating 3-pointer from beyond the halfcourt line to give the Atlanta Hawks a 124-121 victory over the Jazz on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Aggies avoid upset at San Jose State, 85-78
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - In their first game of the season as a ranked team, the Utah State basketball team avoided a major upset at San Jose State Tuesday night. Mason Falslev had 17 points and seven rebounds, Tucker Anderson added 15 points and No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sensabaugh pours in 34 as Jazz crush Miami, 136-100
item.description: MIAMI (AP) - Brice Sensabaugh scored a career-high 34 points, John Collins finished with 24, and the Utah Jazz snapped a five-game losing streak with a 136-100 rout over the Miami Heat on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: White nails career-high nine 3s, scores 33 as Bulls beat Knicks 139-126 after honoring Rose
item.description: Coby White made a career-high nine 3-pointers and scored 33 points, Zach LaVine also had 33 and the Chicago Bulls beat the New York Knicks 139-126 after honoring former MVP Derrick Rose on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
